dimensions.name: Prompt Completeness
dimensions.rationale: Identity >50 words. Instructions >200 words with 4 numbered sections. Tool guidance with Linear GraphQL, GitHub REST, Slack API specs. Three examples (branch creation, PR merged, weekly report). Error handling with rate limits, auth, branch conflicts.

dimensions.name: Tool-Prompt Alignment
dimensions.rationale: http_request mapped to Linear GraphQL, GitHub REST v3 (refs API), Slack Web API. file_read/write for linear_state.json and velocity_log.json. No phantom tools.

dimensions.name: Trigger Coherence
dimensions.rationale: Two triggers: 2-min polling for issue/PR sync + Friday 5PM schedule for weekly velocity report. Both match corresponding prompt workflow sections.

dimensions.name: Connector Coverage
dimensions.rationale: Linear, GitHub, Slack all have complete connector definitions with credential fields and setup instructions.

dimensions.name: Memory Design
dimensions.rationale: Two local files: linear_state.json for sync state (last poll, known issues), velocity_log.json as append-only event log. Custom section documents branch naming convention.

dimensions.name: Error Handling
dimensions.rationale: Rate limits with header-based backoff, auth failures with user notification, branch creation conflicts (422), Linear state transition failures with closest-match fallback, state corruption with backup and reinit.

dimensions.name: Use Case Fidelity
dimensions.rationale: All 3 use_case_flows covered: issue-to-branch creation, PR merged-to-issue closure with cycle time, weekly velocity report with per-assignee breakdown.

dimensions.name: Value Clarity
dimensions.rationale: Eliminates manual status syncing between Linear and GitHub. Automated branch creation, ticket closure, and velocity reporting with trend analysis.

dimensions.name: Execution Feasibility
dimensions.rationale: Standard APIs: Linear GraphQL, GitHub REST, Slack Web API. All connectors have builtin definitions. Triggers are standard cron.

dimensions.name: Differentiation
dimensions.rationale: Cycle time calculation, velocity trend analysis with week-over-week comparison, branch naming from issue identifiers. Good reasoning beyond simple automation.

dimensions.name: Variable Necessity
dimensions.rationale: Adoption questions cover owner, repo, team key, channel, default branch, and auto-create toggle. All genuinely needed.

dimensions.name: Default Quality
dimensions.rationale: GitHub owner/repo have generic defaults requiring user input. Linear team key (ENG) and Slack channel (#engineering) have reasonable defaults. Default branch (main) is sensible.

issues: No adoption_questions existed before fix - GitHub repo, Linear team, Slack channel, default branch all needed user-specific configuration

template: Added 6 adoption_questions: github_owner, github_repo, linear_team_key, slack_engineering_channel, default_branch, auto_create_branches
